title: World Cup 2026: 10-man Belgium stumbles to a 0-0 draw with Iran as Group G looks wide open
original_text:
World Cup 2026: 10-man Belgium stumbles to a 0-0 draw with Iran as Group G looks wide open
DR Congo predicted lineup and team news vs Portugal
DR Congo, formerly known as Zaire, begins its FIFA World Cup campaign on Wednesday with a match against Portugal, marking its return to the tournament after a long 52-year absence.
DR Congo head into this historic tournament with mixed results from their recent friendlies. They managed a commendable 0-0 draw against Denmark but were narrowly beaten 2-1 by Chile in their final warm-up match.
Advertisement
Despite these results, their qualification campaign highlighted their determination and resilience. The Leopards knocked out Nigeria on penalties and secured a 1-0 extra-time victory over Jamaica to book their place at the World Cup.
DR Congo team news
The squad has had to deal with a late setback, as defender Rocky Bushiri was forced to withdraw due to an Achilles injury. In response, head coach Sébastien Desabre called up midfielder Aaron Tshibola to maintain squad depth.
Even with this change, DR Congo still has strong defensive options, including players with English Premier League experience.
Advertisement
Aaron Wan-Bissaka and Axel Tuanzebe are both fit and expected to play crucial roles in handling Portugal’s attacking threats.
In midfield, Noah Sadiki and Samuel Moutoussamy will focus on breaking up play and providing defensive support.
Going forward, DR Congo will depend on the pace and movement of Yoane Wissa, while Cédric Bakambu is set to lead the attack as the main striker.
DR Congo predicted lineup
Possible DR Congo starting XI: Mpasi; Wan-Bissaka, Kapuadi, Tuanzebe, Mbemba, Masuaku; Moutoussamy, Sadiki, Mukau; Wissa, Bakambu
When will the match kick off?
The match will kick off at 6pm BST on Wednesday, 17th June.
Advertisement
How to watch Portugal vs DR Congo?
For viewers in the UK, the Portugal vs. DR Congo match will be available to watch live and free-to-air on BBC One.
